frontend: Next.js App Router + React + Radix Themes + Tailwind plumbing
storage: SQLite or Postgres-compatible durable store for dataset releases, issues, approvals, agent runs, and replay timelines
real_data_source: Synthetic/desensitized医保 dataset fixtures with source manifests and secure trusted data-space migration path
deployment: local dev for G3, public deploy later via Cloudflare Pages or Vercel after reality review

deploy.reason: simple public judge URL for a Next.js product surface

live_provider_smoke.evidence: .hunter/agent-runs.json with two distinct live inputs and outputs during G3

no_wallet_exception: This is a server-issued医保 workflow state product, not Web3/ZK/chain. The user does not own, mint, trade, pay, attest, or sign an onchain result.
sponsor_technology_not_applicable_reason: The contest sponsor primitive is医保 trusted data and Agent orchestration rather than a named SDK; domain data/rules are core in the product state loop.
tailwind_rejection: Tailwind-only or default shadcn would look like a generic AI/admin shell; use app-owned release-state components.
visual_differentiation_note: trusted data release workstation, not fraud dashboard or lineage wall
forbidden_lookalikes: AI purple landing page, command center, audit console, permission matrix, data-quality score dashboard
mobile_primary_flow: mutation chips and CTA first, proof bottom sheet after action
desktop_parity_plan: desktop and mobile keep same state labels, replay, and proof links
non_tailwind_visual_signature: release-state rail, mutation chips, consequence preview strip, replay split
delivery_mode_reason: Final contest requires live system operation and defense, so materials should support a live pitch rather than only a combined video.
why_this_stack: Next.js gives server-side provider boundaries, Radix gives accessible product controls, and durable storage supports state-changing Agent evidence.
